title: Acilepis pulneyensis
intro: Acilepis pulneyensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, native to the Western Ghats of India. The plant is known for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of various diseases such as arthritis and diabetes. The bioactive constituents of Acilepis pulneyensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Herbal preparations made from Acilepis pulneyensis include infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions.
taxonomy: Acilepis pulneyensis is a flowering plant species. It belongs to the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. The genus Acilepis is a small group of plants native to the Western Ghats in India. Acilepis pulneyensis is specifically found in the Nilgiri Hills region of Tamil Nadu in India. Its classification places it within the family Asteraceae, tribe Anthemideae.
